Gomes Paes Da Silva
NOBILIARIOS DE FAMÍLIAS DE PORTUGAL.
Nobiliário de Famílias de Portugal - Tomo XXVII - Silvas - Página 9

km.Santarém, Santarém, Santarém, Portugal: សានតារeម, សានតារeម, ព័រទុយហ្កាល់

zh.Santarém, Santarém, Santarém, Portugal: 葡萄牙桑塔雷姆桑塔雷姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Señor Gomez Paez De Silva於1130年出生在葡萄牙時，他的父親Don Payo Gutierrez da Silva60歲，母親D. Sancha Annes de Mentor50歲。他和Urraca Nunes Velho至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1170年在他的家鄉享年40歲，埋葬在葡萄牙。

zh-hans.Santarém, Santarém, Santarém, Portugal: 葡萄牙桑塔雷姆桑塔雷姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Señor Gomez Paez De Silva于1130年出生在葡萄牙时，他的父亲Don Payo Gutierrez da Silva60岁，母亲D. Sancha Annes de Mentor50岁。他和Urraca Nunes Velho至少有4个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1170年在他的家乡享年40岁，埋葬在葡萄牙。

nameMeanings.name: Paez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Jorge, Carlos, Jesus, Pedro, Rafael, Manuel, Miguel, Raul, Ricardo.
Spanish (Páez): Castilianized form of Portuguese and Galician Pais .

variants.name: Pais
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Fernando, Jose, Alberto, Ana, Ramon, Adelino, Alina, Altino, Angel, Armando, Armindo, Catalina.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Sebastiao, Serafim.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Silvio, Antonino, Ateo, Elio, Guido, Lia.

Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the personal name Paio, an equivalent of Spanish Pelayo . This surname is also found in western India (Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Goa), where it was taken by Portuguese colonists.
Italian (southern): probably of Spanish origin (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Silva
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Jesus, Mario, Fernando, Francisco, Pedro, Roberto, Miguel.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Joaquim, Agostinho, Duarte, Henrique, Serafim, Vasco, Ilidio, Adao, Afonso, Altair.

Portuguese, Galician, and Italian: habitational name from any of the many places called Silva in Portugal, Galicia (Spain), and Italy, or a topographic name from (Old) Portuguese and (Old) Galician silva ‘wood’, later ‘bramble’. Compare Da Silva and Silba .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Iberian surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Da Silva
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Fernando, Francisco, Luiz, Mario, Jorge, Julio, Marcelo, Roberto.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo, Duarte, Agostinho, Henrique, Marcio, Nelio, Albano, Altair, Amadeu, Catarina.
Portuguese and Galician: variant of Silva , with the addition of da, a shortened form of the preposition de ‘from’ and the feminine definite article a ‘the’.

variants.name: Silba
meanings: Hispanic and Italian (Apulia): altered form of Portuguese, Galician, and Italian   Silva  .

variants.name: Silveira
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Luis, Fernando, Carlos, Pedro, Francisco, Mario, Ana, Joaquin, Jorge, Adolfo.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Altair, Duarte, Henrique, Joaquim, Serafim.

Portuguese and Galician: topographic name from silveira ‘woodland’, a collective derivative of silva (see Silva ); or a habitational name from any of the places called Silveira in Lugo and Pontevedra provinces, Galicia (Spain). Compare Da Silveira , Selvera , and Silvera .

variants.name: Sylva
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Fernande, Mathieu.
Altered form of Portuguese, Galician, and Italian   Silva  . Outside the US this surname is most common in Senegal and the Gambia, where it is of Portuguese origin.

variants.name: Silvas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Manuel, Raul, Jose, Lupe, Roberto, Alfredo, Armando, Domingo, Emilio.
Portuguese and Galician: topographic name from silvas, plural of silva ‘thicket, bramble’. See Silva .
Americanized form of Hungarian Szilvás: occupational name for a grower or seller of soft fruits, derived from szilva ‘plum’.

nameMeanings.name: Gomez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘complete’ in Hebrew), borne by a son of Japheth and grandson of Noah. It is in occasional use in the United States. Gomer is also an English surname, derived from an Old English personal name composed of gōd ‘good’ + mǣr ‘famous’, and the given name may also be a transferred use of this.

en.lifeSummary: When Señor Gomez Paez De Silva was born in 1130, in Portugal, his father, Don Payo Gutierrez da Silva, was 60 and his mother, D. Sancha Annes de Mentor, was 50. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Urraca Nunes Velho. He died in 1170, in his hometown, at the age of 40, and was buried in Portugal.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Señor Gomez Paez De Silva est né en 1130, à Portugal, son père, Don Payo Gutierrez da Silva, avait 60 ans et sa mère, D. Sancha Annes de Mentor, avait 50 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Urraca Nunes Velho. Il est décédé en 1170, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Portugal.

de.lifeSummary: Als Señor Gomez Paez De Silva 1130 in Portugal geboren wurde, war sein Vater Don Payo Gutierrez da Silva 60 und seine Mutter D. Sancha Annes de Mentor 50. Er hatte mit Urraca Nunes Velho mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1170 im Alter von 40 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Portugal begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Señor Gomez Paez De Silva è nato il nel 1130 nel Portogallo, il suo padre, Don Payo Gutierrez da Silva, aveva 60 anni e il suo madre, D. Sancha Annes de Mentor, aveva 50 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Urraca Nunes Velho. Morì nel 1170, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolto a Portogallo.

ja.Santarém, Santarém, Santarém, Portugal: ポルトガルサンターeムサンターeム
ja.lifeSummary: Señor Gomez Paez De Silvaが1130年にポルトガルで生まれた時，父Don Payo Gutierrez da Silvaは60歳，母D. Sancha Annes de Mentorは50歳であった。彼はUrraca Nunes Velhoとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1170年に故郷で40歳で死去し，ポルトガルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Señor Gomez Paez De Silva(이)가 1130년 포르투갈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Don Payo Gutierrez da Silva(은)는 60세였고, 그의 어머니 D. Sancha Annes de Mentor(은)는 50세였다. 그는 Urraca Nunes Velho(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1170년 그의 고향에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 포르투갈에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Señor Gomez Paez De Silva nasceu em 1130, em Portugal, seu pai, Don Payo Gutierrez da Silva, tinha 60 anos e sua mãe, D. Sancha Annes de Mentor, tinha 50 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas com Urraca Nunes Velho. Ele faleceu em 1170, em sua cidade natal, com 40 anos, e foi sepultado em Portugal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Señor Gomez Paez De Silva родился в 1130 г. в местности Португалия, его отцу по имени Don Payo Gutierrez da Silva было 60 лет, а матери, которую звали D. Sancha Annes de Mentor, 50. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Urraca Nunes Velho. Он умер в 1170 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности Португалия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Señor Gomez Paez De Silva nació en 1130, en Portugal, su papá, Don Payo Gutierrez da Silva, tenía 60 años y su mamá, D. Sancha Annes de Mentor, tenía 50 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas con Urraca Nunes Velho. Murió en 1170, en su pueblo, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultado en Portugal.
